title: Labor force participation rate by age
subtitle: Share of the working-age population (ages 15 and over) who are economically active (employed or unemployed).
caption: Global labor force participation rates from 1990 to 2024 display distinct trajectories across four age cohorts. The line for the 25-54 age group remains the highest throughout the period, holding steady near 80% before a slight dip around 2020 and a subsequent recovery. In contrast, the 15-24 age group shows a consistent downward slope, starting near 60% in 1990 and falling to just above 40% by the end of the timeline. The 55-64 cohort follows an upward path, beginning near 53% and rising to approximately 61%, crossing above the 15-24 line around the year 2000. Participation for those aged 65 and older stays relatively flat, hovering just above 20% with a minor dip in 2020. The chart shows a widening gap between the prime working-age population and younger workers, while older workers increasingly remain in the labor force.
